content.text:
Now I have a clear picture of the codebase. Let me update `report.py` and `test_report.py`:

1. `build_report` needs to detect numeric columns, compute their median with `statistics.median`, and include them in a `column_stats` key.
2. The existing `test_row_count_only` does an exact equality check — its rows have a numeric column `"a"`, so it'll need updating once `column_stats` is added.

content.text:
All 12 tests pass and ruff is clean. Here's a summary of every change made:

---

### `src/csvstats/report.py`

| What | Why |
|---|---|
| `import statistics` (stdlib) | Provides `statistics.median`, no new dependency |
| `_column_names(rows)` helper | Collects column keys in insertion order across all rows |
| Numeric detection loop in `build_report` | Attempts `float()` on every value in each column; skips the column if any value raises `ValueError`/`TypeError` |
| `summary["column_stats"]` | Added only when at least one numeric column exists; each entry has a `"median"` key produced by `statistics.median(floats)` |

### `tests/test_report.py`

| Test | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `test_row_count_only` (updated) | Fixed the now-broken exact-equality assertion; checks `column_stats["a"]["median"] == 1.5` |
| `test_median_numeric_columns` | Odd-length case using the fixture's data shape; confirms non-numeric column `"name"` is absent |
| `test_median_even_count` | Even-length case where median is the mean of the two middle values |
| `test_median_mixed_column_skipped` | Column with one non-numeric value must be excluded entirely |
| `test_no_column_stats_for_empty_rows` | Empty input produces only `{"row_count": 0}` |

result:
All 12 tests pass and ruff is clean. Here's a summary of every change made:

---

### `src/csvstats/report.py`

| What | Why |
|---|---|
| `import statistics` (stdlib) | Provides `statistics.median`, no new dependency |
| `_column_names(rows)` helper | Collects column keys in insertion order across all rows |
| Numeric detection loop in `build_report` | Attempts `float()` on every value in each column; skips the column if any value raises `ValueError`/`TypeError` |
| `summary["column_stats"]` | Added only when at least one numeric column exists; each entry has a `"median"` key produced by `statistics.median(floats)` |

### `tests/test_report.py`

| Test | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `test_row_count_only` (updated) | Fixed the now-broken exact-equality assertion; checks `column_stats["a"]["median"] == 1.5` |
| `test_median_numeric_columns` | Odd-length case using the fixture's data shape; confirms non-numeric column `"name"` is absent |
| `test_median_even_count` | Even-length case where median is the mean of the two middle values |
| `test_median_mixed_column_skipped` | Column with one non-numeric value must be excluded entirely |
| `test_no_column_stats_for_empty_rows` | Empty input produces only `{"row_count": 0}` |
